entry.title: Washington Post: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Zelensky accuses Russia of ‘barbarism’ after Odessa attack; 2 Americans killed in Donbas
  • His city occupied, a Ukrainian mayor fights to save his community
  • Ukraine wants more ‘game-changer’ HIMARS. The U.S. says it’s complicated.
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Odessa strike ‘undermines’ grain deal, U.S. says; 2 Americans die in Donbas region
  • Russia attacks Odessa port a day after signing grain deal, Ukraine says
  • Pope Francis heads to Canada with apology on the agenda
  • As Europe’s heat wave melts roads, Tour de France races into an uncertain future
  • How will the Ukraine grain deal affect the global food crisis?
  • Man opens fire on Philippine campus, killing 3 people
  • Houthi report says flash floods in Yemen leave 9 dead
  • Pope heads to Canada as Indigenous groups seek full apology
  • 2 apparent homophobic attacks reported after Berlin Pride
  • China launches one of 2 lab modules to join space station
  • Russia says strike on Ukrainian port hit military targets
  • Palestinians: Israeli forces kill 2 in West Bank gun battle
  • Russia FM visits Egypt, part of Africa trip amid Ukraine war
  • 315 kids, adults shelter at school to escape Haiti gang war
  • Pope's Indigenous tour signals a rethink of mission legacy
  • 9-year-old survived Iowa campground shooting that killed family, mayor says
  • Fire officials order 6,000 people to evacuate from Yosemite area
  • 2 dead, 1 missing after flash floods hit fire-scarred areas of N.M.
  • Biden ‘likely’ has BA.5 coronavirus variant, White House says
  • 5 economic forces behind the Fed’s next rate hike decision
  • For creators, community is the new follower count
  • Laughter Is a Weapon Putin Can’t Fire Back
  • Is Russia Letting Up on Energy? Don’t Trust Putin
  • Stuck In Air Travel Hell? Blame the Long Shadow of Covid
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means
  • Why China’s Developers Are Facing Mortgage Boycotts
  • Neither side is going to win the abortion fight with outlier cases
  • Now we know the truth on what Trump sought to obscure about Jan. 6
  • Senators, pass the Respect for Marriage Act
  • As inmates languish, Prince George’s County judges turn a blind eye
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 24 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 24 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-24 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong’s monetary base is strong enough to uphold US dollar peg despite recent capital outflows, finance chief says
  • Hong Kong’s MTR Corp apologises to family of elderly man found dead in station toilet hours after missing report filed, vows to investigate
  • Learn Chinese history to cultivate national identity and avoid being divided by foreign forces, Hong Kong security chief tells youngsters
  • Coronavirus: travellers must complete online declaration before flight to Hong Kong in new rule to speed up airport processing; 4,250 cases logged
  • Tugboat crew of Hong Kong’s troubled Jumbo Floating Restaurant in ‘dire situation’ under confinement at mainland Chinese port
  • Mid-Autumn Festival: Fortum & Mason returns with its collection of luxury mooncakes for Hong Kong customers
  • A different take on Chinese migration to California – and a new way to look at Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong in talks with Beijing to ease cross-border data flow as new rules threaten city’s gateway status
  • Hong Kong to build fewer subsidised tiny flats in future, but they will still be available for potential buyers, new housing chief says
  • Former Huawei smartphone sub-brand Honor said to withdraw team from India amid geopolitical tensions
  • Taiwan left divided by US ex-defence chief’s call for longer compulsory military service
  • China’s social media giants to require influencers to disclose agency names on profile pages
  • China has become more ‘aggressive and dangerous’ to US, and its allies says chair of Joint Chiefs of Staff Mark Milley
  • China sends Wentian lab module into space in a first for manned Tiangong station
  • Macau casinos reopen with dim business outlook amid travel curbs, as outlook for year calls for 35 per cent revenue drop
  • South Korea’s new KF-21 fighter jet no match for China’s J-20, analyst says
  • China faces US$6.5 trillion green financing gap to reach 2060 net-zero carbon emissions goal, WEF report finds
  • Can India follow China’s path to labour-intensive manufacturing as population rises but jobs dwindle?
  • China turns to durian diplomacy to boost ties with Southeast Asia
  • Tencent makes a move to turn WeChat’s short-video function Channels into a money pot through ads and e-commerce tool
  • China successfully tests pilotless helicopter launched from ship
  • 10 killed at Gansu coal mine after mountain collapse, says state media
  • China sends food aid to drought-stricken Horn of Africa as accusations fly
  • US$50 billion Russian-Chinese joint venture to build passenger jet at risk as cracks appear in partnership
  • Chinese region of Xinjiang warns of flood risk amid ongoing heatwave
  • South Korea emerges as fastest-growing arms exporter in the world
  • Khmer Rouge survivor in the US opens up on escaping genocide in father-daughter podcast
  • Ukraine war: Philippine bakeries shrink pandesal also known ‘poor man’s bread’ as inflation bites
  • Political pressure is crippling efforts to stave off an economic crisis in Pakistan. Will it become the next Sri Lanka?
  • Vietnam’s ‘coral grave’ at Nha Trang Bay a wake-up call on climate change destruction
  • Coronavirus: Japan’s daily Covid-19 cases surpass 200,000 for first time due to new Omicron variants
  • How the narrative of an all-powerful government hurts China’s image at home and abroad
  • Sri Lanka’s Ranil Wickremesinghe is a schemer and the West’s stooge. Not the man to lead it out of crisis
  • Malaysia’s Johor royals linked to forests felled for oil palm plantations without full approval
  • Thailand’s first monkeypox patient found after fleeing to Cambodia
  • Ukraine war: outraged by Russian strike on Odesa, Ukraine still prepares to resume grain exports
  • Brexit blamed as UK faces logjam at Channel port of Dover
  • California declares state of emergency over wildfire near Yosemite
  • Tell us where Russian troops are living, Ukraine asks citizens in key region seized by Russia
  • Protesters take to the streets in Britain to decry climate change after record heatwave
  • Iran says its intelligence agents dismantled group with ties to Israel’s Mossad spy agency
  • Joe Biden has sore throat and body aches, but Covid-19 symptoms continue to improve, says doctor
  • WHO declares monkeypox a global emergency, sounding the health agency’s highest alarm
  • Taps running dry in Mexico’s wealthy city of Monterrey; ‘like we’ve gone back in time’
  • Greece tourist resort evacuated as wildfire destroys Lesbos island homes
  • Heed the warnings of a global economic crisis from those in the know – and ignore those saying everything’s fine
  • Forget Kendall Jenner’s tequila and try sotol, raicilla or bacanora – 3 lesser known agave spirits
  • He finds Bollywood film locations and has visited 140 countries: meet Ramji Natarajan, the man behind the places you see on screen
  • Life coach reveals how a near-death experience shipwrecked at sea gave her the courage to change course
  • For the love of the game: the Hong Kong athletes who risked career, family and Covid-19 to achieve their sporting dreams
  • Who is Tia Ray? Meet the Chinese singer with one of the bestselling singles in the world
  • Brigitte Lin’s first gender-neutral role, Sally Yeh’s butch Beijing Opera performer make Tsui Hark’s 1986 period comedy Peking Opera Blues highly enjoyable
  • What drew three women to bear Elon Musk children besides his wealth? Women’s reproductive priorities explained
  • 6 British royals who survived attempted assassinations: Queen Elizabeth, Prince Charles and Queen Victoria have been fired at – while Princess Anne famously resisted her would-be kidnapper
  • Inside Pippa Middleton and James Matthews’ happy family: Kate Middleton’s sister and her billionaire husband just had their third baby … so what’s their married life like?
  • Inside Donald and Ivana Trump’s ‘starter’ mansion: they bought the 20,000 sq ft Connecticut home after getting married, then Ivana won it in their divorce settlement before selling it for millions
  • Meet Anna Weyant, the ‘millennial Botticelli’ making millions: the Canadian-born artist rose to fame on Instagram and was signed by Gagosian – but is she really dating a 77-year-old gallery founder?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

  • Team fuses AI, robotics to decide ideal cultivation step for iPS cells
  • Global warming study: 5-year droughts could become the norm
  • Comic C'moA digital manga site set to build market abroad
  • EDITORIAL: Diplomacy must be a vital key in strengthening Japan’s security
  • VOX POPULI: Living in a world where inflation will be with us for years to come
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
